title: PortfolioExposureRule ignores the open current position

evidence.quote: The rule sums only open positions found in record.Trades.
A position is appended to Trades only after exit; a new open entry is stored in currentPosition.
The test comments that Operate only adds to Trades after close, so there are never open trades in Trades, and it makes no assertion.

reasoning: Under the public TradingRecord workflow, the only live exposure is record.CurrentPosition(), while record.Trades contains completed positions. The exposure rule therefore sees zero exposure for ordinary open positions and cannot enforce its limit.
reproduction: Open a BUY position with Amount=200 and Price=100, then evaluate NewPortfolioExposureRule(1.0); exposure should be 200%, but IsSatisfied returns false.
recommendation: Include record.CurrentPosition().CostBasis() when it is open, and only include open positions from Trades if the model is changed to store them there as well.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included exposure test documents the mismatch but does not assert that an oversized current position should trigger the rule.
suggestedRegressionTest: Open a current position whose cost basis exceeds TotalValue*MaxExposure and assert PortfolioExposureRule is satisfied.
minimumFixScope: pkg/trading/risk_management.go and the portfolio exposure tests.
